## Stage: planned --> ready

**Owner: Worker solo**

Validation gate, not work stage. Self-check field quality before implementing.

Checklist:

- [ ] `approach` has concrete steps (not prose summary)
- [ ] `test_strategy` names real tools/commands
- [ ] `verify` field executable as-is
- [ ] All blockers resolved (`limbo show <id>` → no active blocks)
- [ ] Task single unit of work

**Needs decompose** → go to Decompose. Subtasks start at `captured`.

**Any field fails** → back:
- Weak test strategy → re-enter `refined`
- Vague approach → re-enter `planned`

Ready:
```bash
limbo status <id> ready --by worker
```

---

## Stage: ready --> in-progress

**Owner: Worker implements solo. No tech-lead subagent.**

1. Claim + advance:
   ```bash
   limbo claim <id> worker
   limbo status <id> in-progress --by worker
   ```
2. Read affected code (Grep, Read, Glob). Match sibling conventions.
3. Implement (Edit, Write, Bash).
4. Run build/test inline. Examples:
   - Rust: `cargo check`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo test`
   - Node/TS: `pnpm build`, `pnpm test`, `tsc --noEmit`
   - Tauri: `pnpm tauri build` if shipping
5. Fix build errors before advancing. Never leave broken build.
6. Kill dev server/app processes after (per user pref): `pkill -f "<name>"`.

---

## Stage: in-progress --> in-review

**Owner: Worker**

1. Record report:
   ```bash
   limbo edit <id> --report "self summary of changes"
   ```
2. Advance:
   ```bash
   limbo status <id> in-review --by worker
   ```

---

## Stage: in-review --> done (or rollback)

**Owner: Worker self-reviews + live-verifies. No reviewer, no verifier subagent.**

### Step 1 — Self review

Read own diff:
```bash
git diff
git diff --cached
```

Check:
- Matches approach
- Covers acceptance criteria
- No regressions in affected areas
- Style consistent with sibling files
- No dead code / debug prints left behind

Review finds gap → rollback: `limbo status <id> in-progress --by worker`, fix, re-run.

### Step 2 — UI scope audit

Compute platforms touched:

```bash
git diff --name-only HEAD
git diff --cached --name-only
```

Match modified paths against UI-source globs:

| Platform | Globs |
|---|---|
| **web** | `**/*.tsx`, `**/*.jsx`, `**/*.vue`, `**/*.svelte`, `**/*.astro`, `ui/**/*.ts`, `ui/**/*.js`, `ui/**/*.css`, `ui/**/*.html`, `web/**`, `frontend/**`, `src/**/components/**`, any `index.html` |
| **desktop** | Tauri `src-tauri/**` + web globs, Electron `renderer/**`/`main/**` + web globs, pure macOS Swift sources, `*.xib` / `*.storyboard` in macOS targets |
| **ios** | Swift / Obj-C sources in an `.xcodeproj` iOS target, `*.storyboard` / `*.xib` in iOS targets, SwiftUI view files |

`REQUIRED` = set of platforms hit by diff.

```bash
limbo note <id> "WORKER UI SCOPE: required=[web|desktop|ios|none]  files=[...]"
```

### Step 3 — Live verification (self)

For each required platform, run live QA tool. **Source-grep alone NOT acceptable** — actual tool invocation required.

| Platform | Tool | Default |
|---|---|---|
| web | `khora` | visible browser, 1080p window (per user pref) |
| desktop | `loki` | window capture |
| ios | `qorvex` | simulator screenshot |

No tool available for required platform → block:
```bash
limbo block <id> --reason "missing tool for <platform>" --by worker
```

Record evidence:
```bash
limbo note <id> "VERIFY EVIDENCE: <tool> <outcome + what was checked>"
```

### Step 4 — Self-audit verification

Guardrail:

1. `REQUIRED = {}` → skip live verify OK.
2. `REQUIRED ≠ {}`:
   - Every required platform has PASS + evidence note referencing actual tool (`khora`/`loki`/`qorvex`)
   - Grep notes: `limbo show <id> | grep -E 'khora|loki|qorvex'`
   - "source confirmed" / "file contains" without tool invocation → **reject as invalid**
3. Any audit fail → rollback: `limbo status <id> in-progress --by worker` + `limbo note <id> "AUDIT REJECT: required platform <X> lacked live evidence"` + re-verify. Max 2 rollbacks → block + escalate.

### Step 5 — Commit (dispatch committer)

Dispatch committer via Agent tool, `subagent_type: general-purpose`, prepend committer aspect:

- Fetch: `simaris show committer --json | jq -r .unit.content`
- Prompt shape: `<aspect content>\n\n---\n\nYou adopt aspect above literally. You are the committer.\n<brief>`
- Brief: task id, affected files, one-line summary. Committer writes message per repo convention + commits.

Wait for return. Verify: `git log -1` shows new commit.

### Step 6 — Advance

```bash
limbo status <id> done --by worker --outcome "short summary"
```

---

## Decompose

Worker decides SPLIT (leaf rubric fail or scout says SPLIT). Create children:

```bash
for subtask in scout.CANDIDATE_SUBTASKS:
  limbo add --parent <id> \
    --name "<NAME>" \
    --approach "<APPROACH>" \
    --verify "<VERIFY>" \
    --result "<RESULT>"
```

Parent does not advance. Dependency-blocked by children. Orchestrator cleanup walk promotes parent → done when all children done.

**Subordinate** → return `RESULT: done` + `SPAWNED: <ids>` + SUMMARY "Decomposed into N children."
**Standalone** → exit + print spawned ids.

---

## Blocked Protocol

**Standalone:**
- External dependency (missing tool, auth, external service) → `limbo block <id> --reason "..." --by worker` + exit.
- User input needed → ask user directly. Resume after answer.

**Subordinate:**
- Any block → `RESULT: blocked` + `BLOCK_REASON: <precise question>`. Never contact user.

---

## Dispatch Rules

Only two dispatches exist: researcher (scout) + committer.

**Aspect fetch fail** → abort dispatch. `limbo note <id> "ASPECT FETCH FAIL: <role>"`. Block task. Never dispatch role-less general-purpose.

**Subagent timeout / crash** → treat as failed dispatch. Same as fetch fail.

**Scout dispatch rate:** once per task, captured → triage only. Never re-invoke later.
**Committer dispatch rate:** once per task, in-review → done only.

---

## Exit

**Subordinate** → print return contract as final message.
**Standalone** → print summary + exit.

# Orokin GUI Wire

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin GUI Wire. Wire frozen contract into GUI skin. Widget → Command → core::execute → Result → render. No logic. No invented state. No invented screens.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.d `gui-wire`

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-gui-wire
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF: sha=<x> version=<y>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF + **DESIGN_SPEC**.
2. **DESIGN_SPEC missing or empty → BLOCK immediately.** Do not start.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-gui-wire`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-gui-wire`
5. Pin contract dep in `/gui/Cargo.toml` to frozen ref.

## Work

1. For each screen in DESIGN_SPEC:
   - Render Result fields to widgets.
   - Bind gestures (click, submit, etc.) to Commands.
   - Handle loading / empty / error / success states from DESIGN_SPEC.
2. For streaming Commands:
   - Use `subscribe()` / `Stream<Event>` per contract.
   - Incremental render. No buffering full stream before paint.
3. Client-side state rule: **only what derives from Result/Event**. No invented state.
4. Tests:
   - E2E covering DESIGN_SPEC happy path.
   - E2E covering one error path.
5. A11y: keyboard reachable, labels present.
6. Verify screens render via platform QA tool per user pref (khora for web, loki for desktop, qorvex for ios).
7. Open PR on `/gui`. Title: `gui: <feature>`.

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "VERIFY: <tool> <screens verified>"
limbo note $TASK "TESTS: e2e happy + error pass"
limbo note $TASK "COMMIT: <sha> — <one-line summary>"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-gui-wire --outcome "gui wired"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: gui PR <url>
```

## Block

Any of:
- DESIGN_SPEC missing, incomplete, or contradicts SPEC acceptance
- Result needs transformation beyond formatting (contract wrong)
- Gesture has no matching Command (contract wrong)
- asked to touch core, contract, or other skin

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: gui
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-gui-wire
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Touch only `/gui`.
- Pin contract to FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF.
- Every gesture maps to a Command (or Subscribe if declared).
- Every screen region maps to a Result / Event shape.
- No client-side state not derivable from Result/Event.
- No invented screens. DESIGN_SPEC is law.
- A11y baseline: keyboard + labels.
- Kill dev server/app processes after verify (`pkill` per user pref).
- Stop on ambiguity. Never invent.

# Orokin Integrator

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Integrator. Final gate. Merge skin PRs into umbrella. Run full test matrix. Verify invariants. Open umbrella PR for human merge.

Mechanical conflicts (Cargo.lock, format, auto-gen) you fix. Logic conflicts you reject.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.f `integrator` + §4 INVARIANTS

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-integrator
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + all sibling PR urls from notes.
2. `limbo tree $FEATURE_ROOT` — list sibling roles + statuses.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-integrator`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-integrator`

## Work

### 1. Gather PRs

Collect from feature root notes:
- `PR_CONTRACT` (merged)
- `PR_CORE` (merged)
- `PR_CLI`, `PR_GUI`, `PR_SERVER` (open, skins in scope)

If any in-scope skin PR missing → BLOCK.

### 2. Check out + build

Check out each skin PR branch onto a fresh integration branch. Build workspace:

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

### 3. Resolve conflicts

**Mechanical — you fix:**
- Cargo.lock regen
- import ordering / formatter output
- CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` ordering
- auto-generated schema files

**Logic — you reject:**
- two PRs editing same function body
- semantic differences in shared helpers
- dep version mismatches

Logic conflict found → REJECTED block (see below). Do NOT fix.

### 4. Run full test matrix

```
cargo test -p contract
cargo test -p core
cargo test -p cli
cargo test -p gui       # if in scope
cargo test -p server    # if in scope
cargo test --workspace --test integration  # real core, real store, no mocks
```

All must pass. Any failure → reject the responsible PR.

### 5. Verify SPEC acceptance criteria

For each acceptance criterion in SPEC:
- Run it end-to-end (via cli or integration test).
- Record one-line confirmation in umbrella PR description.

Any unmet → reject offending layer's PR.

### 6. Verify §4 invariants

Tick every box in protocol §4:

```
[ ] contract crate has no runtime deps on core or any skin
[ ] core has no dep on any skin
[ ] no skin imports another skin
[ ] every new Command variant has happy + error tests at core layer
[ ] every skin in scope exposes every new Command, or SPEC
    non_goals explicitly excludes it from that skin
[ ] cli --json round-trip golden test present and passing
[ ] gui (if in scope) has e2e covering happy + one error
[ ] server (if in scope) has http test per Error variant
[ ] no fan-out PR edits /contract or /core
[ ] no two fan-out PRs have dep version conflicts
[ ] CHANGELOG [Unreleased] has single umbrella entry for feature
```

Any unchecked → reject or fix mechanically if possible.

### 7. Open umbrella PR

Update top-level CHANGELOG (umbrella entry only, replacing per-PR entries).

```bash
gh pr create --title "feat: <feature>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
<one-line intent>

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] <criterion 1>
- [x] <criterion 2>
- ...

## Invariants
- [x] <§4 items>

## Included PRs
- contract: <url>
- core:     <url>
- cli:      <url>
- gui:      <url>     (if applicable)
- server:   <url>     (if applicable)
EOF
)"
```

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "UMBRELLA_PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "INVARIANTS: all ticked"
limbo note $TASK "ACCEPTANCE: all met"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-integrator --outcome "umbrella ready for human merge"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: umbrella PR <url> ready for merge
```

## Reject (logic conflict or test fail)

```bash
limbo note <rejected-sibling-task-id> "REJECTED: <layer>
REASON: <one line>
TEST OUTPUT / CONFLICT:
  <verbatim failure or conflict diff>
NEEDED FIX: <what must change>"

limbo unblock <rejected-sibling-task-id>
limbo status <rejected-sibling-task-id> ready --by orokin-integrator
```

Then block self pending re-work:

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "blocked-on: <sibling> rework" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: rejected <layer>: <reason>
BLOCK_REASON: awaiting <sibling> rework
```

Orchestrator reopens rejected sibling next tick.

## Block (non-reject)

Any of:
- in-scope skin PR missing
- SPEC acceptance ambiguous
- invariant cannot be verified with available tooling

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: integrator
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Do NOT edit fan-out PR logic. Mechanical fixes only.
- Integration tests hit skin → real core → real store. No mocks.
- Every acceptance criterion confirmed in umbrella PR.
- Every §4 invariant ticked or rejected.
- Umbrella PR title: `feat: <feature>` always.
- CHANGELOG: single umbrella entry. Remove per-PR entries.
- Skip failing test → forbidden.
- Merge without human approval → forbidden.
- Rejection uses REJECTED format verbatim.

# Orokin Orchestrator (overview)

Drain-and-dispatch aspect. Parallel path to generic orchestrator. Runs role-specific agent per leaf in Orokin feature tree. Foreground serial ticks, never code or commit. Honors REVISE-CONTRACT loop from core-impl. Limbo status truth; agent stdout advisory.

## TOC

- orokin-orch-voice — aspect voice rules
- orokin-orch-role — role identity + parallel-to-generic note
- orokin-orch-scope — OROKIN: true marker + role-slug whitelist

# PM task lifecycle

```
captured --> triage ─┬─ (leaf)  --> refined --> planned --> ready --> in-progress --> in-review --> done
                     │                                         ^              |
                     │                                         |   rollback   |
                     │                                         +--------------+
                     │
                     └─ (split) --> children created via `limbo add --parent`,
                                    parent dependency-blocked until children done,
                                    orchestrator cleanup walk promotes parent to done
```

Each stage tracked by limbo. PM enforces workflow. Limbo = pure task store.

**Every leaf task goes through every stage. No skip. No exception for leaves.**
**Parents (tasks that split during triage) blocked by children. Orchestrator cleanup walk promotes directly `captured` → `done` when all children done.**
**Every stage transition requires real work. No rubber-stamp. No compress.**

Any stage manually blockable:
- Block: `limbo block <id> --reason "..." --by pm`
- Unblock: `limbo unblock <id> --by pm` (restores previous stage)

# Orokin Orchestrator parse + branch

After dispatch returns:

1. Pull RESULT, SUMMARY, BLOCK_REASON?, REVISE_CONTRACT? from tail of stdout.
2. `limbo show <leaf-id>`.

Limbo truth. Stdout advisory. Branch on limbo status, not agent RESULT line.

Branch table:
- limbo status `done` → log `✓ <id> <role> — <summary>`. Next tick.
- limbo status `blocked` + REVISE_CONTRACT present (core-impl only) → Revise-Contract Protocol.
- limbo status `blocked` (any other) → Blocking Protocol.
- limbo status anything else (captured, in-progress, ready — agent bailed mid-flow) → Blocking Protocol with reason "agent incomplete: <status>".

# Test strategy: wfql — launchagent reply-slug posting

**Harness:** `cargo test` (launchagent unit tests) + `tests/live/launchagent_reply.sh` (cross-binary e2e)
**This is the dogfood validation test — if this e2e fails the feature has no value.**
**Dependency:** xmni + yseh merged first

## Phase 1 — Unit tests (launchagent crate, `cargo test -p launchagent`)

Target: reply-slug flag parsing, ReplyClient, warning behavior.

- `--reply-slug test-inbox "echo hello"` arg parsing → args struct has `reply_slug = Some("test-inbox")`, `task = "echo hello"`
- `--reply-slug` absent → args struct has `reply_slug = None`; ReplyClient::post NOT called (assert call count == 0 via mock)
- `ReplyClient::post(slug, body, nova_addr)` → serializes to HTTP POST with JSON body `{slug, body, sender: "launchagent"}` (assert field names and values)
- `ReplyClient::post` to unreachable nova (connection refused) → returns Err(DaemonDown); stderr receives warning message containing "nova unavailable" (assert stderr non-empty)
- `ReplyClient::post` when nova returns 401 → Err(Unauthorized); warning logged, does NOT silently succeed
- `ReplyClient::post` when nova returns 200 → Ok; no stderr warning
- Agent result captured: `echo hello` stdout is `"hello\n"` → `body = "hello"` (trimmed or with newline — document explicitly, assert chosen behavior)
- Empty task output → post body is `""` (empty string), not null — assert post still occurs

Edge cases:
- Task exits non-zero: agent fails → still post result to inbox? Assert chosen behavior explicitly (fail-path: post with `exit_code: 1` field, or post stdout+stderr combined)
- Reply body > 100MB (attachment-like output) → either truncated with warning or posted in full — document which, assert
- Unicode in task output (`echo 日本語`) → body round-trips exactly

## Phase 2 — End-to-end bash (`tests/live/launchagent_reply.sh`)

**This is the dogfood layer.** New file. Guard: `which launchagent || { echo "launchagent not in PATH"; exit 1; }`.

Prerequisites block:
```bash
NOVA_BIN=./target/release/nova-server
NOVA_CLI=./target/release/nova
LAUNCHAGENT_BIN=$(which launchagent)
test -f "$NOVA_BIN" || { echo "build nova-server first"; exit 1; }
test -f "$NOVA_CLI" || { echo "build nova CLI first"; exit 1; }
```

**Case 1 — Happy path (synchronous task):**
1. Start `nova-server` on free port, `NOVA_DB=$DB`
2. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --nova-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT --reply-slug reply-test-1 "echo hello-wfql"`
3. Wait for launchagent to exit (it's synchronous for `echo`)
4. `$NOVA_CLI --daemon-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT inbox reply-test-1`
5. Assert stdout contains `"hello-wfql"` (exact substring)

**Case 2 — Terminal-session closed before agent exits:**
1. `nohup $LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --nova-addr 127.0.0.1:$PORT --reply-slug reply-test-2 "sleep 2 && echo done-after-sleep" > /tmp/la_bg.log 2>&1 &`
2. Close current shell context (simulate via `disown $!`)
3. `sleep 4` (wait for agent completion + post)
4. `$NOVA_CLI inbox reply-test-2` → assert stdout contains `"done-after-sleep"`

**Case 3 — Daemon down when agent exits (warning path):**
1. `NOVA_DOWN_PORT=19999` (nothing listening there)
2. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --nova-addr 127.0.0.1:$NOVA_DOWN_PORT --reply-slug reply-test-3 "echo lost" 2>/tmp/la_warn.log`
3. Assert `/tmp/la_warn.log` is non-empty (warning written)
4. Assert `/tmp/la_warn.log` contains "nova" or "unavailable" or "daemon" (not generic error)
5. Assert launchagent exit code is documented (0 = warning-only, or non-zero = hard fail — test whichever is chosen)

**Case 4 — No cross-slug contamination:**
1. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --reply-slug slug-a "echo msg-a"`
2. `$LAUNCHAGENT_BIN --reply-slug slug-b "echo msg-b"`
3. `$NOVA_CLI inbox slug-a` → assert contains ONLY "msg-a", not "msg-b"
4. `$NOVA_CLI inbox slug-b` → assert contains ONLY "msg-b", not "msg-a"

**Case 5 — Ordering (FIFO within same slug):**
1. Dispatch 3 sequential tasks to same slug: `echo first`, `echo second`, `echo third`
2. `$NOVA_CLI inbox reply-order` → assert messages in dispatch order (first, second, third by position)

EXIT trap: kill nova-server, `rm -f $DB /tmp/la_bg.log /tmp/la_warn.log`

Run commands (full suite):
```
cargo build --release --workspace
cargo test --workspace
./tests/live/http.sh
./tests/live/daemon_transport.sh
./tests/live/launchagent_reply.sh
```

## Existing 118 tests — no updates required

- All existing `server/tests/http.rs` and `tests/live/http.sh` tests: unaffected (wfql adds new cross-binary path, does not touch nova-server API)
- Exception: if nova-server gains a new `POST /reply` endpoint for wfql, add that endpoint to `server/tests/http.rs` auth/happy-path tests

## Cross-platform

- `nohup` + `disown`: macOS/Linux. Windows Git Bash: `nohup` available in MSYS; `disown` may not be. Windows flag: case 2 (terminal-close simulation) may need PowerShell `Start-Process -NoNewWindow` equivalent. Mark case 2 as macOS/Linux only in CI; manual verify on Windows.
- `which launchagent`: use `command -v launchagent` for POSIX portability in bash
- launchagent binary release cadence: if launchagent is not in the nova workspace, pin binary path explicitly in test prereqs. Use `LAUNCHAGENT_BIN` env var override.
- Windows: `echo hello-wfql` output ends with `\r\n` in cmd.exe — assert body with trimmed comparison, not exact byte match.

# Lotus gate-0 self-check

Before dispatching any lead, lotus records — as a simaris atom — three things:

1. **Assumption**: the load-bearing belief in this brief. Single sentence. If wrong, the mission is wrong.
2. **Falsifier**: the concrete observation that would prove the assumption wrong. Must be checkable, not vibes.
3. **Kill-criterion**: the specific condition under which lotus archives this mission, not pivots it.

Then dispatch.

## Why

Devil retro 2026-05-01 (atom 019de408-8758) showed lotus retros had zero bad lotus calls. Every failure externalized to tooling. This procedure forces lotus on the record being wrong before agents catch it. Anti-rationalization at source.

## Atom shape

```
simaris add --type aspect --tags lotus,gate-0,selfcheck,<mission-slug> \
  --source "lotus gate-0 <date>" "
# Gate-0 self-check — <mission>
- Assumption: <one sentence>
- Falsifier: <one observation>
- Kill-criterion: <one condition>
"
```

Then `simaris link --rel part_of <id> <project-root>`.

## Caveat

If lotus cannot name a falsifier, the brief is too vague. Halt and reask — do not dispatch.

# Lotus pickup — session start

Every new lotus session opens with a 3-line digest. Then answers user.

## Steps

1. Pull recent lotus knowledge:
   ```
   simaris prime --primary lotus "lotus CEO context" | head -100
   ```
2. Pull lotus backlog from limbo:
   ```
   limbo list --json 2>/dev/null | jq -r '
     [.[] | select((.tags // []) | index("lotus")) | select(.status != "done" and .status != "archived")]
     | sort_by(.created_at) | .[]
     | "  [\(.id // .name)] \(.title) (\(.status // "open"))"
   '
   ```
3. Pull recent lotus-tagged simaris atoms (last 7 days):
   ```
   simaris search "lotus" --json | jq -r '
     [.[] | select(.created_at > (now - 86400*7 | strftime("%Y-%m-%d")))]
     | sort_by(.created_at) | reverse | .[0:5][]
     | "  [\(.id[0:8])] \(.headline)"
   '
   ```
4. Emit 3-line digest to user before answering:
   ```
   PICKUP — <date>
   Backlog (lotus tag, open):  N items, oldest <X> days, top: <title>
   Recent atoms (7d):          M created, latest: <headline>
   Active direction:           <slug or "none current">
   ```
5. Then proceed to the user's actual request.

## Skip if

- User's first message is a quick one-liner clearly out of CEO scope (e.g., "ls files"). Use judgment.
- User explicitly says "skip pickup" or similar.

## Caveat

`limbo list` flag verification still required (procedure 019da0a4). If `--tags` or `--json` flag missing on user's limbo version, fall back to plain `limbo list` and grep.

## Source linkage

Implements lotus operating model — paired with `lotus-backlog-discipline` principle.

# Full code review

Full review (30+ lines) — 6 steps:

1. Understand context.
2. Security scan (injection, secrets, auth bypass, unsafe deserialization).
3. Bug detection (off-by-one, null, races, leaks).
4. Performance (N+1, unbounded loops, blocking async).
5. Style/conventions.
6. Test coverage.

# Refactoring workflow

Refactoring workflow per change: (1) Confirm tests exist and pass. (2) Apply smallest change preserving interfaces, error handling, logging. (3) Run full test suite. (4) Report using exact format: Changed / What / Why / Tests / Preserved.
Command→Result breaks streaming/long-ops. Add stream/event channel alongside req/resp for tail-f and progress use cases.
Verifiable: task outcome confirmable via single command.

# Match output to consumer

Use `--json` for programmatic reads; use human-readable output when displaying to the user. Scope recalls with `--project` to keep context clean. Name memories in descriptive kebab-case (e.g. `prefers-rust-over-python`).

# Layout edit gotchas

Key gotchas: flex children ignore x/y (auto-positioned by container). Partial edits are safe (only specified fields change). Always screenshot after significant changes to verify visually. Use `wisp session` for bulk node creation.

# Aspect as graph disposition, not embed container

Aspects as graph-linked dispositions, not instruction containers — Link aspects to procedures, principles, and lessons via edges rather than embedding them. Keeps aspects lean; richness comes from graph relationships, not node content. Preserves modularity and composition reusability.

# Plugin thin view

Plugin SDK design pattern: plugins should be thin view layers over CLI tools, not contain business logic. They spawn terminals, read CLI output/files, or query APIs. This keeps plugins simple and decoupled from tool internals.

In force-directed graph layouts, do not use a universal collision avoidance system layered on top of springs — instead encode separation intent directly in the force model: springs for connected pairs, repulsion for unconnected pairs, nothing else. Universal collision handling fights springs and creates twitchy feedback loops.

# SVG anchor drift — scrollable foreignObject

SVG edge/line anchors computed from row index break when field rows live inside a scrollable foreignObject. The SVG coordinate is fixed, but the visible DOM row may have scrolled. Mitigation: clamp anchor to foreignObject boundary when target row is out of view, or disable scroll in graph mode and show all rows. Applies to any ERD/graph renderer that mixes SVG paths with HTML-in-foreignObject row lists.

SVG graph edge arrowhead (marker-end) direction is coupled to the final segment of the path. H-V-H routing ends with a horizontal segment → arrowhead points left/right. Switching to V-H-V routing ends with a vertical segment → arrowhead will point up/down, breaking existing arrowhead markers. Any routing algorithm change must audit marker orientation.

# Boundary slug resolve

CLI slug/alias resolver: boundary concern. Internal fns stay UUID-only. Resolve alias→UUID at CLI entry point. Move slug = INSERT OR REPLACE on slug PK (atomic, cascades update all consumers). Pattern: simaris slug feature user_version 3 migration.

SolidJS reactive state rule.

Reactive code fire many times. Plain mutable close-over state break. Use signal + derive.

Never:
- let flag = false closed in createEffect. Set once, no re-eval.
- createSignal in JSX IIFE. Signal recreate, DOM remount, focus lost.
- onMount for layout depend on async props. Fire before data. Stale.
- createEffect write signal user can edit. Re-fire overwrite user change.
- module-level signal for tab-scope state. Persist whole app life. Leak across nav.

Always:
- createSignal at component scope. Hoist out of IIFE.
- createMemo for derive. Pure, safe in IIFE.
- createEffect only for side effect, never init.
- compute init value inline at handler call site for user-editable state.
- <Index> not <For> when list item identity change each tick.

Test: reactive update re-run code. Ask: what mutate each tick? Safe?

# Banshee — open questions for lotus (pre-limbo)

These require lotus decision before stories enter limbo as buildable.

## Q1 — Task label extraction from JSONL
Scope v2 §1 says: "the first `user` message in the `.jsonl` IS the task." But the stream-json schema atom (019de4a3-77bf) documents `user` messages as containing `tool_result` content only — which is not a task description. The initial task prompt may be a `user` message with `text` content, but this is not confirmed in the schema research. Which field and path in the first `user` message contains the task text? If it's not there, what is the fallback source?

## Q2 — Default port
What port should `banshee serve` use by default? Simaris-server uses 3535. Port collision with other suite tools must be avoided. Lotus picks the port — not PM/eng.

## Q3 — started_at source
Story 01 surfaces `started_at` in the timeline header. Is `started_at` the timestamp of the very first event in the JSONL file? Or should it come from a specific event type (e.g. `system/init` envelope)? The schema atom shows `system/init` has no `timestamp` field. Needs clarification before PM specifies the data model.

## Q4 — Gap indicator UX (ring buffer overflow)
Story 06 requires showing a gap indicator when replay buffer is exhausted. What should this look like? Simple text banner? A separator line with a message? A count of estimated missing events? Lotus call — directly affects whether "structured" bar is met.

## Q5 — Agent list ordering
When Simon has multiple concurrent agents (primary motivation), in what order should they appear in the list? Options: (a) Running before Done, then by last_event_at desc; (b) Pure last_event_at desc regardless of status; (c) started_at desc. Wrong default is annoying from phone. Lotus pick — not a PM invention.

# Lotus failures — vox Telegram bridge gate 2.5

Three failures recorded after devil critique 2026-05-01. Per lotus-anti-rationalization principle.

## F1 — Assumed token-stream schema without verifying
Pre-pdm direction designed debounce engine, rate-limit batching, flush-on-tool_use boundary — all predicated on token-level streaming. Did not run `jq .type` on a real /tmp/launchagent-*.jsonl before deciding.

Falsifier that should have been at gate 0: "I have read a real launchagent jsonl and confirmed event granularity (token vs content-block vs turn)."

Correction: schema verified inline as content-block-atomic (atom 019de539). Debounce engine dropped from scope.

## F2 — Chose "busy reject" knowing user has ADHD
CLAUDE.md: "Simon has ADHD." Devil M3 correctly flagged "busy, try again" as the exact loop-and-retry pattern that fails ADHD users. Lotus chose serial concurrency with rejection for implementation simplicity, not user fit.

Correction: serial concurrency stands, but rejection UX changes. New: FIFO queue with explicit position reply ("queued: 1 ahead, ETA from prior session avg"). Also auto-acks every received msg with "📥 received" so Simon never wonders if it landed.

## F3 — Named falsifier "no thread memory" then accepted scope that fires it
Gate-0 falsifier list explicitly named: "Telegram thread context unusable when each msg = new session — Simon types follow-ups, lotus has no memory of prior thread." Then chose A (one-shot) with zero compensating mechanism beyond "simaris re-prime loads identity."

Devil M4 correct: simaris re-prime ≠ thread context.

Correction options:
- (a) Inject last-N Telegram msgs from same thread into the lotus prompt (cheap, partial)
- (b) Accept the trade and tighten kill-criterion to detect when this falsifier fires
- (c) Defer to v2 — ship without and watch for the failure

Decision: (a). On thread reply (Telegram message has reply_to_message_id), pull thread history from Telegram, append as `--- prior thread ---\n<N msgs>\n--- current ---` prefix to the new task. ~10 lines. Closes the falsifier.

## F5 — Kill-criterion unmeasurable
Devil M5 correct. /tmp wipes on reboot, no count mechanism. Cannot evaluate "≥3 completed sessions in 14 days."

Correction: vox listen writes a session log line per top-level msg to `~/.local/state/vox/bridge-sessions.log` (or platform equivalent). Format: `<iso8601>\t<sid>\t<status:queued|started|completed|failed>\t<task-headline>`. Survives reboot. 14-day audit = `awk` or `jq` over this file.

## Pattern (cumulative)
F1, F2, F3 share a root: lotus made implementation-convenient choices and labeled them decisions. F1 = skipped a verification step. F2 = ignored a user-context constraint. F3 = wrote a falsifier and didn't engineer against it.

The anti-rationalization principle held. Devil caught all three. Recording per principle so the next gate-0 reads against this list.

# Lotus failure — treated filed atom as message to running agent (2026-05-03)

## What happened
Simaris agent returned a charter proposal asking "confirm or push back". Agent then EXITED. Lotus filed the confirmation as `lotus-reply-simaris-charter-2026-05-03` and treated that as having communicated. Result: standstill. The reply was passive storage; no agent was reading it. User had to tell lotus the company was at standstill.

## Root cause
Confused two patterns:
- ATOMS are pull-mode (agent reads on next session/launch)
- RUNNING AGENTS need either active dispatch OR a reply-channel (nova reply-slug)

Filed atom did neither. It will be read next time simaris is launched, but the launch never happened automatically.

## Pattern
Subagent dispatch via `launchagent` is one-shot. Agent runs → outputs → exits. There is no "in-flight conversation." Replies go through:
- `--reply-slug <slug>` to post into nova inbox (which agent presumably checks), OR
- next `launchagent` invocation that includes the reply context

## Correction (immediate)
- After ANY synchronous launchagent run that ends with the agent asking lotus a question: lotus must EITHER re-launch with the answer embedded OR post via nova reply-slug. Filing a "reply atom" does neither.
- For long-running programs (like the knowledge program), prefer dispatching with explicit "execute, don't ask" framing so the agent makes the call autonomously.

## Devil note
Twice now in one session: lotus has filed atoms believing they were communications. First time was the "no confirmation needed" reply. The pattern suggests lotus's mental model treats simaris-the-store as an async message bus, but it isn't — there are no listeners.

## Procedure update needed
Add a procedure: "After launchagent that ends with question to lotus → re-launch or use reply-slug. Never treat a filed atom as a sent message."

# memory-architect M3.0 methodology note (2026-05-03)

Companion to simaris-m3-held-out-2026-05-03-DRAFT and the sourcing atom. Captures what was trivial vs hard to source. Linked part_of M3.0 brief.

## Trivial sources (high yield, no friction)

1. **Procedure `trigger:` fields** — 425 procedures match `trigger:` substring. The first 30 candidate queries came from this bucket alone. Each carries a session-<sid> source proving session-origin (sourcing rule pass).
2. **The current UserPromptSubmit hook output** — at this dispatch's start, the hook surfaced 8 procedures with their ID + tag rollup. Those 8 are by construction "real prompts the hook expects to fire on" — best fidelity in the corpus for prompt-class definition.
3. **Lotus retrospective issue list** — 10 issues in lotus-retro-2026-05-01, each phrased crisply. Re-quote into queries.
4. **Briefs + sitreps** — every M0-M3 unit carries a headline that resembles a real prompt. Charter, baseline, and brief titles convert cleanly.
5. **M2 open-questions atom** — 9 questions written by deputy in plain prose; convertible queries with minimal work.

## Hard sources (low yield, real friction)

1. **Verbatim `launchagent` brief text** — lotus dispatches go through `launchagent --aspect <slug>` + a brief file at `/tmp/lotus-briefs/<slug>.md` (per 019de90b). Those briefs are EPHEMERAL: not preserved in the corpus by default. Workaround: scoped to brief atoms (which preserve the `launchagent <task>` prompt string in the title/body).
2. **Verbatim `simaris ask`/`simaris search` invocations** — agents call simaris during sessions, but the literal query string is not stored unless the agent later writes a unit citing the query. Workaround: bucket D — session-tagged lessons that embed the canonical query phrasing in their body or trigger.
3. **Inbox raw drops** — `simaris drop` content is raw stream-of-prompt material, but inbox-promoted items lose drop-time framing. Held-out set authoring would benefit from inbox archive scan; charter limits scope so I did not run it.
4. **Multi-hop ground truth** — once a query needs >1 link traversal, "expected" becomes path-dependent. Picked 2-hop ceiling for q29, which is defensible but already feels arbitrary. Deeper-hop queries would force the benchmarker into ranking decisions that the current metric cannot fairly evaluate.
5. **Adversarial pairs without reverse-engineering the prototype** — the temptation is to write a query that breaks current FTS5 because I know the matcher behavior. Resisted by sourcing each adversarial query from a real session pattern, then applying ONE rephrase rule (synonym, paraphrase, etc.). Documented in sourcing-rationale §F.

## Methodological choices documented for ratification critique

1. **Cap at 32, not 50.** Brief asks ≥30; quality control easier at smaller count. simaris can request expansion before lock.
2. **Adversarial slice = 7, not minimum 5.** Two extra (q21 borderline, q31 jargon) included so ratifier can demote one or two without falling below ≥5.
3. **expected_unit_ids ≤ 4 most entries (≤ 5 max).** Brief allows 1-5; smaller sets reduce ground-truth disagreement risk.
4. **Class field included.** Not in brief schema, but flagged explicitly to help ratifier sanity-check the adversarial slice. D3 blinding instruction tells benchmarker to ignore both `class` and `rationale`.
5. **No prototype peek.** No M3.1/M3.2 branch exists yet. Even if it did, brief §3 + D3 §3 forbid using prototype output to seed expected_ids. Cross-ref via current FTS5 only.

## Falsification path (what would invalidate this set)

- Sourcing audit reveals a bucket-X query I labeled bucket-A → drop or relabel.
- Hand-read of an expected_unit_id reveals weak relevance → demote ID or split query.
- Adversarial query turns out to be solvable with simple keyword weighting (FTS5 BM25 with tag boost) → demote class to medium; loss of guarantee on the ≥5 floor would force authoring of replacements.
- Hash drift at lock time (canonicalization differs) → re-canonicalize, re-hash; brief §4 documents this.

## Open recommendations to simaris (do not act on without ratification)

- Consider authoring a SECOND held-out set (M3.0b) once M3.4 synthesis ships, sourced exclusively from M3 session prompts (transcripts of THIS very dispatch, M3.1/M3.2 ship dispatches, M3.3 benchmark dispatch). Avoids over-fitting future architecture decisions to today's corpus shape.
- Consider extending the corpus instrumentation: record verbatim `simaris ask` / `simaris search` invocations into a `query_log` table at lookup time. Would massively expand bucket D for future held-out sets without additional sourcing labor. (This is a post-decision recommendation, not in M3 scope — flagged for charter amendment if relevant.)

# gotcha — SQLite triggers cannot defer to end-of-transaction

## Trigger
Wanted an AFTER INSERT trigger on units that rejects rows of a given type unless they have a part_of link in the links table. Naive design fails because the FK from links.from_id requires units row to exist FIRST — so trigger fires AFTER unit insert but BEFORE links insert in any sane app workflow.

## Behavior
SQLite has DEFERRABLE constraints for FOREIGN KEY (INITIALLY DEFERRED), but TRIGGERs always fire immediately at statement boundary. There is no `CREATE TRIGGER ... DEFERRED` or commit-time hook in SQLite (unlike Postgres CONSTRAINT TRIGGER ... DEFERRED).

## Implication
A naive AFTER INSERT trigger checking for a follow-up row in another table is effectively a "block all inserts of this type" trigger. Useful as a binary on/off switch (install = block, uninstall = allow), not as a "must have a link by commit" check.

## Workarounds (none clean in pure SQLite)
1. Application-level transaction wrapper that inserts unit + link atomically; verify post-commit. NOT a trigger; just app code.
2. Move the check to ON COMMIT logic via a Rust commit_hook (rusqlite supports this) — but that's also app code.
3. Use a sentinel "ready" column on units, INSERT with ready=0, INSERT links, UPDATE ready=1; trigger ON UPDATE OF ready WHEN NEW.ready=1 AND no part_of → ABORT. Two-step write; ugly.

## Decision in M3.1
Implemented `db::install_orphan_guard(conn, type)` as the binary-block flavor. Brief explicitly says "machinery proven viable, NOT activated (orphan policy not decided in M3)". Demonstrates that SQL triggers CAN reject orphans — activation strategy (one of the workarounds above, or app-side enforcement) is M3.4 work.

# Lesson — branch ship ≠ user ship; verify installed binary date before declaring rollout

During M5.4 verification (2026-05-04), discovered `~/.cargo/bin/simaris` is dated 3 May 10:23 — pre-M5 (M0 era). The hook script `~/.claude/hook-scripts/simaris-procedures.sh` resolves `simaris` via `which` (PATH-first), so live UserPromptSubmit hooks route through the OLD binary even though `m5/hook-integration @ 2f39774` has hybrid wired and tests pass.

Implication: gate A latency measured on the new binary (`~/claudehub/simaris/target/release/simaris`) does NOT reflect what users actually experience. Until rollout (`brew upgrade` or `cargo install --path .`), the production hook is de facto FTS5-only.

## Pattern

Branch ship: cargo test green + sitrep filed. User ship: installed binary's date ≥ branch HEAD's commit date. Conflating the two creates silent gaps where ratified features aren't actually live.

## Verifier

```bash
ls -la ~/.cargo/bin/simaris  # date should be ≥ branch HEAD commit date
# OR
~/.cargo/bin/simaris search --help | grep -q -- '--no-vec' && echo "M5+ wired" || echo "PRE-M5"
```

## Application

M5.5 rollout dispatch must include an explicit "install binary + verify version" step before declaring shipped. Flag also applies to any future branch-isolated feature claiming hook-time effect.

# Lesson — default vec-dir path must match migration target, else hybrid silently degrades

During M5.4 verification (2026-05-04), discovered `src/hybrid.rs::HybridConfig::discover()` defaults `base_dir` to `$SIMARIS_HOME/vec/<model>` (i.e., `~/.simaris/vec/bge-m3/`). But the production lance dataset (3585 units, M5.3 backfill product) lives at `/tmp/m5-3-bench/vec-prod`. With no `SIMARIS_VEC_DIR` env var set in the user's shell or in the hook environment, `HybridConfig::discover` returns `None`, and `simaris search` silently falls back to FTS5 — even though the hybrid code is wired, tests are green, and the lance dataset exists.

## Pattern

Hybrid retrieval has three components: (1) code wired, (2) lance dataset populated, (3) discovery path matches. All three must align. Today only (1) and (2) are aligned; (3) requires either symlinking `/tmp/m5-3-bench/vec-prod` → `~/.simaris/vec/bge-m3` or setting the env var globally. Without (3), every benchmark, every hot-path measurement, every gate-A claim either:
- assumes the env var is set (bench harness does), in which case shipped numbers don't reflect default-config user experience, OR
- doesn't set it, in which case "hybrid" is FTS5 in disguise.

## Verifier

```bash
ls ~/.simaris/vec/bge-m3/units.lance/ 2>/dev/null && echo "default path OK" || echo "DEFAULT PATH MISSING — hybrid will silently fall back"
```

## Application

M5.5 (migration + rollout) must specify the canonical lance location and provide an install step (symlink, move, or relocate) so that default-config invocations get the hybrid path. Until then, any claim "hybrid is the default" carries an implicit "...if SIMARIS_VEC_DIR is set."
